title: Broken CUDA extension imports (apex, apex_C, custom CUDA libs) must be removed before NPU execution
problem_description: Import statements for CUDA-only extension libraries (apex_C, custom fused kernels, etc.) cause ModuleNotFoundError at import time, preventing the entire script from running. These imports execute before any NPU code paths are reached, making them a hard blocker. The apex_C import was present at line 25 of train.py and was labeled as an intentional E2E test injection, but the same pattern occurs in real projects with CUDA-only dependencies.
rough_fix_approach: 1. Scan all import statements for CUDA-only packages (apex, apex_C, custom CUDA extensions, xformers, flash_attn if no NPU equivalent). 2. Remove the import line entirely if the functionality is optional or unused elsewhere. 3. If the functionality IS used downstream (e.g., fused_layer_norm called elsewhere), replace with torch.nn.LayerNorm which torch-npu supports natively. 4. Verify no usage references remain after removal via grep. 5. Do NOT add try/except wrapper imports — they defer failure and create silent CPU fallback behavior.

involved_code_files.role: Training script entry point — contained broken CUDA extension import at line 25, removed during repair

reasoning: Every CUDA-to-NPU migration will encounter CUDA-only extension imports. These are hard blockers that prevent any execution. The removal-and-replace pattern (use native torch equivalents) is universally applicable because torch-npu provides native support for most standard operations (LayerNorm, etc.) that CUDA extensions accelerate.
